senses.gloss: to be offensive to the nose, to smell foul; to stink (especially of decay)
to be offensive to the nose, to smell foul; to stink (especially of decay); to be offensive to the senses, faith, or morals; to be vile, repulsive, or disgusting
to cause to stink, to fester, to putrefy, to decay

etymology.text:
Allegedly cognate to Hebrew נָתַן (nātán, “to give”), Aramaic נָתַן (nātan, “to give”), Aramaic נְתַן (nətan, “to give; to put”), Phoenician 𐤉𐤕𐤍 (ytn, “to give”), Ugaritic 𐎊𐎚𐎐 (ytn, “to give”), Ge'ez ነተን (nätän, “to give”), Akkadian 𒋧 (nadānum, “to give”) by specialization to the meaning “give off smell”. Note the Akkadian spelling comes from Sumerian 𒋧 (šum, “to give”), however it is spelt identically to the Semitic Akkadian 𒋧 (šum, “garlic, onion; potent smelling bulb”) possibly indicating an early semantic connection; compare ثُوم (ṯūm).
More remote connections are with وَتِين (watīn) and أَدَّى (ʔaddā).
